name: cairn
description: Portable agent environment bootstrap — memory, laws, skills, context templates. Works in Claude Code, Antigravity, and Pi (pi.dev). Domain-agnostic. Graduated adoption via tiers.

userClaude.description: User-global agent config directory (env-agnostic name; root for agent-installed content)
userClaude.cowork: probe: workspace-level .claude/ or equivalent user-scoped config; ask user if uncertain

userMemory.description: Persistent cross-session memory root. Claude Code has TWO conventions — detect which one this session uses before resolving. Wrong path = silent-failure install (files land where the agent never reads).

claude-code.note: <project-slug> is the project's absolute path with slashes/colons replaced by hyphens (e.g. C:\Users\winno\projects\foo -> C--Users-winno-projects-foo).
claude-code.resolution: See adopt.md Step 1: probe filesystem for existing memory, or check system-prompt directives, or ask user. Do not default blindly.

pi.note: <projectname> is typically the project root directory basename. Pi's pi-hermes-memory package uses a separate SQLite-backed store; cairn typed-markdown memory lands here independently and does NOT compete with pi-hermes-memory. The directory may not exist until first install — create on demand.
pi.resolution: See adopt.md Step 1: probe for ~/.pi/agent/ marker. Default to user-global unless system-prompt or filesystem signal indicates project-scoped use.

userSkills.description: Skill directory. Claude Code and Pi have TWO conventions each (same pattern as userMemory) — detect which this session uses before resolving. Wrong path = silent-failure install (skills land where the agent never loads them). v0.12+ ships skills as subdirs containing SKILL.md — canonical for both Claude Code AND Pi, which use the identical format.

claude-code.resolution: See adopt.md Step 1: probe filesystem for existing skills, or check session conventions, or ask user. Do not default blindly.

pi.format-note: Identical to Claude Code: subdir + SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name <=64 chars lowercase+hyphens, description <=1024 chars, optional license/compatibility/metadata/allowed-tools). Pi invokes as /skill:<name>; Claude Code invokes as /<name>.
pi.v0.14-note: <project>/.agents/skills/ aligns with cairn's v0.14.0 <project>/agents/ umbrella — Pi loader auto-discovers both .pi/ and .agents/ paths.
pi.resolution: See adopt.md Step 1: probe for ~/.pi/agent/ marker. Default to user-global unless project work specifically benefits from project-scoped install.

projectClaude.description: Project-scoped agent config (applies to current work effort only)
projectClaude.pi: {cwd}/.pi (primary) OR {cwd}/.agents (v0.14.0-aligned alternative; Pi loader recognizes both)

projectRoot.description: Root of the current work effort

files.description: Project context template — fill in per work effort. Read every session.
Top-level memory index — the lookup entry point for all memory
Meta-laws (the schema) + 6 seed laws. The shape is load-bearing; the seeds are scaffolding — replace with your own laws over time.
Reflective check-in — standardizes something capable agents do anyway
Plan-before-execute — standardizes something capable agents do anyway

Pre-emptive intent capture — file a quick thought as a dated note in the project repo. Distinct from /reflect (post-hoc) and from cross-session memory (user-space). Includes the two-destinations disambiguation table.

External-perspective review of a change set by a fresh agent who didn't author it — reads diff PLUS adjacent unchanged files to catch inconsistency-class bugs the work-author missed. Distinct from /reflect (post-hoc same-agent). Named /peer-review to disambiguate from Claude Code's built-in /review skill.
Generate 2-4 alternative framings of the current problem — collaboration skill for rotating the solution space when convergent thinking is stuck
Structure cross-session context relay — collaboration skill for carrying output between agent sessions with minimal loss
Simulate end-user perspective before shipping — collaboration skill for rotating the observer position from builder to user

Detect and load context from a previous session — probes HANDOFF.md, memory at multiple slugs, transcript junctions, recent git activity. Defeats namespace fragmentation in session-to-session handoff.
User memory conventions — always-on background, no citation
Feedback memory conventions — fires discretely, cite [MEM feedback/<name>]
Project memory conventions — decision-shaping, cite [MEM project/<name>]
Reference memory conventions — episodic lookups, cite [MEM reference/<name>]
How skills work in cairn — authoring guide for adding more
Post-install onboarding walk-through — grow out of it once the habitat is familiar
Stale memory/law review — becomes useful as the habitat accumulates entries
Citation frequency report — becomes useful once session history accumulates
File structured feedback to cairn's GitHub repo — cairn-ecosystem-specific (integrates with cairn's own issue tracker)

Artifact-creation skill: write a structured agent execution spec (docs/specs/) with phases, steps, checkpoints, executor handoff. For non-trivial code changes that need an executor handoff. Distinct from /plan (behavioral, conversational) and from /note (single-paragraph intent capture).

Program-of-specs authoring: produce one master coordination doc + N workstream child stubs in docs/specs/ for substantial work that exceeds a single spec's scope. Bakes in no-deferral discipline, cross-cutting axes coverage (telemetry/diagnostics/rollback per workstream), informal axes-sign-off + commit-then-/peer-review at the master gate, and program-internal N-label bookkeeping for review-iteration traceability. Two invocation modes: cold-start and promote-from-spec (when a single /spec outgrew one file). Stubs are elaborated by `/spec --from`.

Round-review verb: trust-but-verify an executor's round of work against a /program-produced master, walk the master's §5 DoD criterion-by-criterion against the diff, surface zero-order findings outside the DoD, and draft (a) R+1 stub specs for each gap PLUS (b) an R+1 round master (SPEC_<NAME>_R<N>_00_MASTER.md) that's self-contained — execution order, round DoD, first-action checklist all inside the file so dispatch is a single one-liner (`/goal execute <round-master-path>`) with no caveats. Closes the orchestration loop: /program → /spec --from (elaborate) → executor → /round-review (produces round master + stubs) → next dispatch → repeat. Loop exits when /round-review writes zero stubs. Do NOT invoke if you authored the executor's implementation (anti-fresh-perspective).

Prompt-evolve verb: author a self-improving, version-controlled prompt for tough tasks done in many iterative passes over partitions (corpus mining, codebase refactor sweeps, doc backfill, bug triage, audits — any task where work doesn't fit in one execution, partitions are obvious, each pass produces both output AND insight, and the insight would otherwise be lost between passes). Primary mode is `--from <SPEC>` — promotes an evolving-prompt deliverable embedded in a /spec into a standalone artifact at `<docs|project/agents>/prompt-evolve/<NAME>_PROMPT.md`. Enforces a canonical 7-phase scaffold (Pre-flight → Inventory → Extract → Write → Verify → Report → **Self-Improvement**) with five non-negotiable enforcement items: (1) Phase 6 absolute-path inline as the edit target, (2) CHANGELOG with v1 entry + v2+ format template, (3) empty TOOL-FAILURE FALLBACK MATRIX section ready to grow, (4) Pending Re-run staging in the FINAL REPORT, (5) idempotence statement. The /prompt-evolve artifact is the only skill output that evolves over its lifetime — Phase 6 self-edits accumulate lessons each pass, and the CHANGELOG IS its institutional memory. Promotion semantics differ from /spec --from <note> and /program --from <spec>: the source spec STAYS IN PLACE (the spec covers more than the prompt; only one deliverable is extracted).

roles.essential: Load-bearing from day one. Habitat doesn't meaningfully function without this. Read first, fill in first.
roles.scaffolding: Grow-into territory. Shape is important (schemas, conventions, discoverability); specific content gets customized over time. Safe to skim at install, impossible to skip entirely.
roles.optional: Ergonomic win. Standardizes something a capable agent does anyway. Delete if you prefer less prompt surface; add back if consistency starts to slip.

tiers.description: Graduated adoption scope. Default install is 'full' (everything). Users who want a minimal, agent-portable habitat can request a lower tier via `adopt ... --tier <name>`. Each tier is cumulative — grow includes seed, structure includes grow, full includes structure.
tiers.seed: Agent-neutral core: CLAUDE.md + memory/MEMORY.md. Cross-session continuity, works with any agent (Claude, GPT, Gemini, local LLM) that can read markdown. The two-file minimum viable habitat.
tiers.grow: Adds durable conventions any careful agent can follow: LAWS.md schema, plan/reflect/note maintenance skills, the collaboration skills (reframe, bridge, advocate), the cross-perspective skills (resume, peer-review). Still reasonably generic — content a non-Claude agent can use with a little orientation.
tiers.structure: Adds cairn's native hygiene loop: typed memory subdirectories, citation conventions, tour/prune/audit skills. Starts to assume cairn-aware tooling and conventions.
tiers.full: Adds cairn-ecosystem integrations like the feedback skill (files issues to cairn's own repo). Claude-Code/Cowork-optimized; default unless user requests a lower tier.

modes.create-if-absent: Write only if destination does not exist. Never overwrite.
modes.merge-append: Append cairn section to existing file (idempotent by marker).
modes.overwrite-with-backup: Back up existing to {dest}.bak, then overwrite. Requires explicit user approval.

url_notes.https://cairn-feedback-591252228833.us-c: Cloud Run direct URL — DNS-independent, always-reliable.
url_notes.https://cairn.winnorton.com/feedback: Custom domain — may not resolve until CNAME propagates after initial setup. Kept as secondary because it's more memorable and survives Cloud Run service renames.

feedback.strategy: first-success: try URLs in array order; on network error, DNS failure, or 5xx, try the next URL before falling through to gh CLI and paste fallback
feedback.description: Primary delivery channel for the /feedback skill. Agents POST structured feedback JSON; endpoint creates a GitHub issue on winnorton/cairn and returns the issue URL. Three-level degradation: these endpoints, then `gh issue create`, then user-paste fallback.

payload_schema.category: gap | bug | skill-idea | clarification | docs | design
payload_schema.title: string, <=120 chars
payload_schema.environment: optional string (Claude Code, Cowork, claude.ai, etc.)
